commit.message:
fix(jetson): fix Docker volume permissions in setup script

Docker container runs as UID created inside the container (agrikd),
which differs from host's agrikd UID. Mounted volumes were only
chown'd to host user, making them inaccessible inside the container.

Fix: After chown, apply chmod to ensure:
- Read-only mounts (config, models): 755 dirs, 644 files
- Read-write mounts (data, logs): 777 dirs
- config.json: 644 after every write (credential injection)
- engine files: 644 after download/conversion

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: -j/--json flag, TOTP hidden by default, search via get-logins, clip/totp accept URL (#4)

* fix: UX improvements — -j/--json flag, TOTP visibility, search via get-logins, clip/totp by URL

- Replace global --format {table,json,tsv} with per-subcommand -j/--json flag.
  The flag can be placed anywhere after the subcommand name, which is the
  standard behaviour for CLI tools.

- output.py: TOTP is now hidden in 'show' output unless -p/--show-password is
  passed. TSV format removed (unused).

- search: Changed from get_database_entries + client-side filter to
  get_logins(query), which works without the 'Allow access to all entries'
  permission in KeePassXC Browser Integration settings. The query is matched
  against URLs/hostnames by KeePassXC.

- totp: Now accepts a URL (like 'show') instead of a UUID. The entry is
  resolved via get_logins and the UUID is passed to get_totp internally.

- clip: 'field' is now a positional argument instead of --field.
  New usage: keepassxc-cli clip <password|username|totp> <url>

- pyproject.toml: bump keepassxc-browser-api requirement to >=0.1.3.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: copy static files to dist/ via CopyWebpackPlugin

The express.static middleware references __dirname/public which resolves
to dist/public in production, but webpack wasn't copying src/public/ to
dist/. This meant the GT3 dashboard and push test GUI were missing in
deployed builds.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: correct expression quoting in release.yml containers list (#1936)

The needs[] bracket expressions used doubled single quotes
(needs[''build-squid'']) inside a YAML literal block (|). In a
literal block there is no YAML quote escaping, so the doubled
quotes were passed verbatim to the expression evaluator, causing:

  Unexpected symbol: 'build-squid''' at position 9

Fix by switching the shell strings from single to double quotes,
allowing single quotes inside ${{ }} expressions to work correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ID

commands, grep patterns)

tracing session continuity

feat(phase1): DSP per output — parametric EQ, brick-wall limiter, latency tuning

EQ (crates/patchbox-core/src/dsp/eq.rs):
- 3-band parametric biquad EQ per output
- RBJ Audio EQ Cookbook, transposed direct-form II
- RT-safe: no allocations, change-detects coefficients via sync()

Limiter (crates/patchbox-core/src/dsp/limiter.rs):
- Brick-wall peak limiter per output
- Envelope follower, asymmetric attack/release
- Hard clamp after envelope — true brick wall

Config (config.rs):
- EqBand, EqConfig, LimiterConfig structs with serde defaults
- per_output_eq, per_output_limiter vecs in PatchboxConfig
- rx_jitter_samples, lead_samples now config fields (default 48, 48)
- All new fields backward-compatible via #[serde(default)]

Audio callback (device.rs):
- PerOutputDsp pre-allocated before RT closure — zero allocs in hot path
- EQ -> limiter chain wired into process()
- GR fed to meter state for WebSocket broadcast

API (api.rs):
- 6 new JWT-protected endpoints: GET/PUT /eq/:id, GET/PUT /limiter/:id
- MeterFrame extended with gr_db field
- build.rs: auto-detect PAM library path (Arch/Debian/RHEL)

Web UI (zone.html, zone.js):
- EQ sliders per band (freq, gain, Q)
- Limiter threshold/attack/release controls
- Live GR meter bar via WebSocket

Latency: rx_jitter_samples 192->48, lead_samples 96->48 (~2-3ms total)

commit.message:
feat(multiplayer): rejoin-or-leave UX for active room conflicts

When createRoom() or joinRoom() returns a CONFLICT because the user
already has an active room, include the blocking room's ID in the error
payload (details.activeRoomId) so the client can offer a recovery path.

LobbyEntryDialog now detects this conflict and replaces the plain error
text with an inline prompt offering two options:
- Rejoin: navigates directly to /play/lobby/{activeRoomId}
- Leave & Continue: calls leaveRoom(), then retries the original action

Error handling: if leaveRoom fails, the error message is shown and the
dialog stays open.

AppError gains a dedicated CONFLICT union member with optional
details?: ConflictDetails. The appError() factory is updated with
typed overloads to preserve type safety at call sites.

Closes Story 17.3 — final story of EPIC 17.

fix(jetson): resolve critical CUDA threading, crash bugs, and edge cases

C1+C2: Replace direct TensorRTInference with InferenceWorkerPool in
gui_app.py — engines now load on dedicated CUDA thread, eliminating
context-affinity violations and shared-buffer race conditions.

C3: Fix TypeError crash in provision.py when device_id is int
(str(device_id)[:8] instead of device_id[:8]).

C4: Remove conflicting WatchdogSec=120 from static agrikd.service
template (setup_jetson.sh generates the actual Docker-based service).

H1: Defer gui_app.py file logging setup until after os.chdir() so
log files are created in the correct directory.

H2: Add basic config validation in gui_app.py main().

H3: Wrap sync_engine.py resp.json() in try/except to prevent crash
on non-JSON responses (e.g., proxy error pages).

H4: Add max_retries=5 to camera._open_with_reconnect() to prevent
infinite blocking during shutdown.

H5: Call pool.shutdown() in gui_app.py closeEvent to release CUDA
device memory on GUI close.

H7: Run health_server.py DB cleanup in background thread to avoid
blocking the Waitress request thread.

M9: Throttle gui_app.py cleanup to every 50 predictions (was every 1).
M10: Guard sync_engine.py device_id int cast with try/except.

commit.message:
End of internship - ASU Fall 2025 (#28)

* update libraries versions in both python and typescript, and several small bug fixes. Added separate startup scripts not requiring the electron app.

* feat: Unified startup system and improved architecture

## Infrastructure
- Add unified start.sh script for managing both local and Docker deployments
- Add docker-compose.yml for container orchestration
- Add Dockerfiles for frontend (Node 18) and backend (Python 3.13)
- Remove obsolete startup scripts (restart_app.sh, start_*.sh)
- Add /datasets/ directory and move dataset generation tools out of backend

## Backend Improvements
- Merge new semantic_comparison.py with enhanced error handling and flexible model loading
- Remove LLM comparison functionality (app/ai/llm_comparison.py)
- Add model management router (/app/routers/models.py) with select/delete/import/list endpoints
- Add /models/translation/* and /models/comparison/* API endpoints
- Fix semantic comparison default model to use full identifier 'sentence-transformers/LaBSE'
- Update comparison API to support new model management

## Frontend Improvements
- Remove all backend lifecycle management from main.ts (kill/start processes)
- Add health check IPC handler calling /health endpoint
- Update TranslationSection to use /health endpoint for status checks
- Remove startBackend function and 'Start Backend' button from UI
- Fix compareArticles service to send correct model name 'sentence-transformers/LaBSE'

## Testing
- Fix all 4 failing tests (mock patches and cache handling)
- Achieve 100% test pass rate (56/56 tests passing)
- Rename semantic_comparison_tests.py to test_semantic_comparison.py
- Convert to proper pytest format with test_* functions
- Fix mock data for translation tests (add 'fr' language support)

## Documentation
- Update README.md with comprehensive start.sh usage documentation
- Add Docker Compose workflow documentation
- Update API endpoints list (remove LLM comparison, add model management)
- Update test statistics (56 tests, 100% passing)
- Update backend and frontend README files
- Fix backend path references in frontend documentation

## Architecture
- Frontend now only monitors backend health, does not manage lifecycle
- Backend lifecycle managed exclusively by start.sh script
- Both local and Docker workflows use same start.sh interface
- Services are independent with proper separation of concerns

This consolidates 4 startup scripts into 1 unified system with full Docker support
and removes all LLM comparison code in favor of semantic comparison only.

Phase 2a: 使用 MixedSingleTaskGP 替代 SingleTaskGP

fix: set correct MSIX Identity Publisher in Package.appxmanifest

full Identity Publisher. Setting the Identity explicitly in the manifest
with the full Lancelot Software, LLC cert subject avoids the O=CN=... error.

Also updated ApplicationPublisher to the plain display name.
